acceptance_criteria.criterion: Shared rule or skill guidance specifies when to update summary.md
acceptance_criteria.evidence: skills/shared/summary-protocol.md has a new 'When to Update' section (lines 27-32) that specifies 4 triggers: after completing a deliverable/sub-task, after settling a design decision, after discovering a key finding/open question, and before requesting step transition. This is in the shared skills layer, available to all steps.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Guidance references CLI commands, not direct file writes
acceptance_criteria.evidence: summary-protocol.md line 23: 'Update via CLI: rws update-summary [name] <section> [--replace] with content on stdin.' Line 24: 'Do NOT edit summary.md directly — use the CLI.' The old version said only 'Update BEFORE requesting step transition' with no CLI reference.

acceptance_criteria.criterion: validate-summary.sh and summary-protocol.md requirements are reconciled
acceptance_criteria.evidence: Key reconciliation: the old summary-protocol.md required '>=2 non-empty bullet points per required section', but validate-summary.sh (both the hook version and the rws validate_summary function) checks for >=1 non-empty line. The new summary-protocol.md now says '>=1 non-empty line per required section', which matches what the hook actually enforces. The step-aware ideate exception (only Open Questions required) is documented in the Required Sections table and implemented in both validate-summary.sh (line 53) and validate_summary() (line 1098-1100).

quality_issues.description: validate-summary.sh was not modified in this row — the reconciliation was achieved by updating summary-protocol.md to match the existing hook behavior. This is the correct approach since the hook was the ground truth and changing the hook would have been riskier.

summary-protocol.md says 'Bullets: - prefix, substantive content (not TBD, None, N/A, TODO)' but neither validate-summary.sh nor validate_summary() actually checks for these placeholder values. They only check for non-empty lines. This is a pre-existing gap, not introduced by this row, but the new protocol perpetuates it.
